# procfunc

> A proc func is a service written as a plain Go function.
> lesiw.io/proc supervises it -- restarts it on failure, retires it
> when it returns, cancels it when its context ends, and places it
> across a cluster -- and moves typed messages between procs with
> at-least-once, FIFO-per-cohort delivery. The same binary runs
> whole on a laptop with the in-memory broker or spread across a
> cluster with a durable one (Postgres today via lesiw.io/procpgx).

## Docs

- [Tour](https://procfunc.io/): scenes for proc.Context supervision
  trees, fail-fast error propagation, Keep restarts, Solo cluster
  singletons, Many slot pools, at-least-once messaging, Query/Serve
  request-reply, durable pipelines, distributed cron, local-to-
  cluster deployment, epoch-gated rolling deploys, and heartbeat
  self-fencing.
- [Field guide](https://procfunc.io/README.md): condensed reference
  for coding agents -- mental model, supervisors, messaging
  contracts, Load/Save semantics, the cron shape, and a traps list.
- [Appendix for Erlang and CSP readers](https://procfunc.io/csp/)
- [API reference](https://pkg.go.dev/lesiw.io/proc)
- [Postgres broker](https://pkg.go.dev/lesiw.io/procpgx)

## Mirrors

- [procfunc.github.io](https://procfunc.github.io/)
